paper_title: Phylogenetic and Biogeographical Analysis of Lejeunea

methods.description: This method involves sampling species across the geographical distribution of the genus Lejeunea, extracting DNA, and preparing it for sequencing and alignment to study phylogenetic relationships.

steps.description: We sampled over the entire geographical distribution of the genus to obtain species from all continents.
steps.searchable_summary: collect species samples from various continents

steps.description: Total genomic DNA was isolated using the Invisorb Spin Plant Mini Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: isolate genomic DNA using Invisorb Spin Plant Mini Kit

steps.description: The rbcL, trnLF and ITS genes were amplified with the PCR protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify rbcL, trnLF, and ITS genes using PCR

steps.description: Bidirectional sequences were generated by an ABI 3730 48 capillary sequencing machine.
Newly generated sequences were assembled and edited with phyDE 0.9971.
steps.searchable_summary: assemble and edit sequences using phyDE

steps.description: The new sequences were integrated into the Lejeunea dataset using BioEdit 5.0.9.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate sequences into dataset using BioEdit

methods.description: This method involves reconstructing phylogenetic relationships using a supermatrix with Maximum Likelihood (ML) and Bayesian Inference (BI) to identify DNA partitions and substitution models.

steps.description: We identified appropriate DNA partitions and corresponding substitution models using PartitionFinder and the Akaike information criterion (AIC).
steps.searchable_summary: identify DNA partitions and substitution models using PartitionFinder

steps.description: The ML analysis was conducted with RAxML-HPC 8.2.8.
Trees were generated by selecting 10 independent runs and the multi-parametric bootstrap option autoMRE, resulting in 300 bootstrap replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: generate trees with bootstrap replicates using autoMRE

steps.description: BI was carried out with MrBayes 3.2.6 using the same dataset, nuclear substitution models, and partitions as those in the ML analysis.
The runs were checked for convergence using the values of the potential reduction factor (PSRF) and effective sample size (ESS) in Tracer 1.7.
steps.searchable_summary: check convergence using PSRF and ESS in Tracer

methods.description: This method estimates molecular dating using Bayesian Inference with BEAST, incorporating fossil calibration and substitution rate calibration.

steps.description: Molecular dating analyses were carried out with the supermatrix using BI as implemented in BEAST 1.8.2.
The fossil †Microlejeunea nyiahae provided a minimum age to constrain the Lejeunea clade.
steps.searchable_summary: use fossil to constrain clade age

steps.description: We conducted four dating analyses using different clock models.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct dating analyses with different clock models

steps.description: All the clock models were compared using Bayes factors calculated with path sampling and stepping-stone sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: compare clock models using Bayes factors

methods.description: This method estimates ancestral areas of distribution for Lejeunea using biogeographical models and reconstructs ancestral ranges on a phylogenetic tree.

steps.description: We defined 9 areas of distribution of Lejeunea for the biogeographical analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: define distribution areas for biogeographical analysis

steps.description: Ancestral ranges were reconstructed on the maximum clade credibility tree using the R package BioGeoBEARS.
We also used the DIVALIKE model for comparison.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ancestral area models using DIVALIKE
